PERSUASION SYSTEM

Basics of The Straight Line Persuasion System	1

-- 2 of 12 --

OVERVIEW:
THE BASIS OF THE STRAIGHT LINE
PERSUASION SYSTEM
>*.#B&%-'1*&#J'(.#K.%+8-+'"(#B9+&.,#'+#-#$%"5.(#$%"3.++#)"%#'(D8.(3'(12#
controlling, and negotiating a sale as well as making yourself known in
such a congruent way that others are compelled to take action for the
1%.-&.%#1""06##<&@+#&*.#,"+&#/"1'3-/2#./.1-(&#=-9#&"#'(D8.(3.#+",.7"09#
because it cuts to the core of what really matters.
Remember, sales is like a straight line—open at one end, closed at
the other, with boundaries above and below the line. Between those
boundaries you develop rapport and gather intelligence by asking
qualifying questions. How you ask those questions determines how much
rapport you build with the client, and ultimately whether or not you make
the sale.
Within the boundaries, you are doing two things:
Establishing instant massive rapport through tonality
and body language.
! 	! 	!"#$%&!'()'!*)++,*'!',!&)'(-*!$%'-..$&-%/-0!1%2!,3'!
what the prospect truly needs, and determine their
true desires.
1
2
JORDAN BELFORT
Basics of The Straight Line Persuasion System	2

-- 3 of 12 --

THE STRAIGHT LINE
BOUNDARIES
RAPPORT
GATHER INTELLIGENCE
(QUALIFYING QUESTIONS)
YOU
The Promised Land:
Closing the Sale
(Leads to Massive
Wealth & Success)
JORDAN BELFORT
Basics of The Straight Line Persuasion System	3

-- 4 of 12 --

STRAIGHT LINE TECHNOLOGY
The tools you learn in the Straight Line System are so powerful they will
make you unstoppable! Once you master these skills, you will be able to
use them in any job you decide to do and in any situation you encounter
in your personal life.
The challenge is that there are unconscious buying triggers and certain
tonal patterns that can make people buy things they shouldn’t, if used the
wrong way.
Therefore, it’s important that you remain ethical:
Never pressure a client to do something they shouldn’t do.
Never pressure a client to buy something they shouldn’t buy.
The Straight Line System is designed to be used in an ethical way only to
encourage people to:
L# M89#&*'(1+#&*.9#+*"8/0#789#&"#',$%"5.#&*.#I8-/'&9#")#&*.'%#/'5.+6
L# #N5.%3",.#"7+&-3/.+#&*-&#-%.#/','&'(1#&*.,#-(0#$%.5.(&'(1#&*.,#)%",#
having the things they should have to make their lives best.
We all have buying obstacles, which are limiting beliefs we have about
buying and making decisions. They hold us back from taking action.
The job of the Straight Line Persuader is to help people overcome these
obstacles so they take action in the direction that serves them most.
1
2
JORDAN BELFORT
Basics of The Straight Line Persuasion System	4

-- 5 of 12 --

THE 3 TENETS OF
THE STRAIGHT LINE
There are three principles of the Straight Line you must know to be effective.
You have to develop instant rapport on both a conscious and
an unconscious level.
The biggest mistake of novice persuaders is that they start talking
about their lives. This is not how you develop rapport with someone!
An essential goal of building rapport is to convince the client
")#&*%..#3%'&'3-/#)-3&+#-7"8&#9"8#='&*'(#&*.#4%+&#)"8%#+.3"(0+#")#
your conversation.
1. 	You’re sharp as a tack. The client must perceive you as a
bright, highly skilled professional who isn’t there just to waste
his time.
2. 	You’re wildly enthusiastic about the product.
Your passion for the product or service your selling and the
value it will bring to your client must be genuine and obvious.
3. 	You’re a force to be reckoned with—an expert in your
1-.24 You’re a highly successful individual who exudes
3"(40.(3.#-(0#$"+'&'5.#.(.%196
These three key factors roll up into one simple fact that the
3/'.(&#,8+&#%.-/'C.#'(#"%0.%#)"%#&*.,#&"#=-(&#&"#0"#78+'(.++#
with you: You are a person who can help the client get
what they want, take control of their lives, and achieve
their goals.
1
JORDAN BELFORT
Basics of The Straight Line Persuasion System	5

-- 6 of 12 --

KEY POINT
You are always developing rapport! Even when the
prospect is signing the order and you are walking out the door,
you’re still developing rapport.
You must learn to gather intelligence effectively.
The problem with many salespeople is that they talk much more
than they listen. Though asking the right questions is crucial to
understanding exactly what it is your client needs, you must truly
listen to their answers to attain that understanding. You won’t
accomplish this by barreling through the conversation and
hardselling your client either. The Straight Line is about
./.1-(3.2#8+'(1#-#3"(+'+&.(&2#+$.3'43#,.&*"0"/"19#&"#,"5.#
the prospect down the straight line, one step at a time.
# 	# 	#O.,.,7.%2#')#9"8@%.#I8-/')9'(1#&*.#$%"+$.3&#-(0#9"8#4(0#"8&#&*.#
product doesn’t suit his needs, end the encounter right there. If your
$%"083&#'+(@&#1""0#)"%#&*.,2#&%9#&"#4(0#"(.#&*-&#'+6##B.(0#&*.,#&"#9"8%#
competitor if that’s what it takes.
Always control the sale by keeping it on the Straight Line.
The Straight Line represents the perfect sale.
For example:
You walk in the door and greet your client, who instantly loves
you and says, “Oh yes everything is great.”
You ask them if they are interested and they say, “Oh, yes,
everything is great.”
2
3
JORDAN BELFORT
Basics of The Straight Line Persuasion System	6

-- 7 of 12 --

You tell them what your product is about and they say, “That
sounds wonderful.”
You say, “It costs X dollars,” and they say, “Great, how do I
get started?”
Now, the perfect sale doesn’t happen too often!
What happens more often is that you try to move the prospect
down the straight line and they keep trying to move you off the
straight line and out into Pluto! The client rarely says what you
want and he doesn’t typically follow your preconceived notion
about how the sale should go.
Every time the prospect tries to take you off the line, your goal
is to keep him on the line (or close to it) in an elegant way that
empowers him. Your goal is to keep the conversation within the
boundaries of the straight line.
# 	#<)#9"8#4(0#&*.#3"(5.%+-&'"(#,"5'(1#"))#&*.#+&%-'1*&#/'(.2#+',$/9#0"#
two things: 1) develop rapport and 2) gather intelligence.
The idea is that, before you go into an encounter, call up an
-)4/'-&.2#"%#,-;.#-(9#,"5.#&"#'(D8.(3.#-#$"&.(&'-/#3/'.(&2#9"8#
need to know the things that you must accomplish in the other
person’s mind in order to get them to say “yes.”
JORDAN BELFORT
Basics of The Straight Line Persuasion System	7

-- 8 of 12 --

THE POWER OF THE THREE 10s
<(#"%0.%#&"#&%8/9#'(D8.(3.#+",."(.2#&*.#)"//"='(1#&*%..#&*'(1+#*-5.#&"#
align in the consumer’s mind.
They have to love you. (They have to trust and connect
with you on an unconscious and conscious level, emotionally
and logically.)
They have to love your product.
THE THREE 10’S
10 	10	10
1.
They must
love your
product
2.
They must
trust and
connect
with you
3.
They must
trust and
connect with
your company
1
2 They have to love your company.
3
JORDAN BELFORT
Basics of The Straight Line Persuasion System	8

-- 9 of 12 --

Goaloriented selling is not about manipulating people to buy
products they shouldn’t buy. It’s about helping people who should
buy your product to make the right decision.
On a scale of one to 10, the client should give an enthusiastic “10!” for
each of these factors. If he has doubts about any one of them, you will
not close the sale. It’s that simple.
How do you get the client to love you, your product, and your company?
By continually strengthening your case as you move down the line.
The secret is to strengthen your case logically and emotionally, not
just emotionally.
You need to develop an airtight logical case for why your product is the
best thing since sliced bread, and you must convince the client that your
$%"083&#/"1'3-//9#4//+#*'+#(..0+6##?"8#+&'//#(..0#&*.#.,"&'"(-/#3",$"(.(&2#
but without logic your sales presentation is dead in the water.
JORDAN BELFORT
Basics of The Straight Line Persuasion System	8

-- 10 of 12 --

THE ACTION THRESHOLD
The Action Threshold is perhaps the most important concept when it
3",.+#&"#'(D8.(3'(1#$."$/.6#P8,-(#7.'(1+#-%.#("&#%-(0",#3%.-&8%.+6#
We don’t do things by accident. We do things for a reason and we
generally have positive intent with what we do.
Now that doesn’t mean that we always do things that empower us.
Sometimes, we make lousy decisions, but we usually make them while
thinking they are in our best interest. The Action Threshold represents this.
The Action Threshold is the unconscious set point
that somebody has to be motivated to taking action.
The Action Threshold is merely someone’s beliefs about buying. Through
life experiences, people build up a resistance, or more accurately limiting
buying beliefs, that lead to a higher set point for buying. Past experiences
buying a car or other product, a negative experience where a company
didn’t stand behind their warranty or even the advice our mothers have
given us while growing up have created voices in our heads that tell us
“Don’t Buy.”
Think about a person’s Action Threshold as a big scale. The fulcrum in the
middle of the scale is the person’s action set point, with all of the positives
(good things that will happen if the decision is made) on the left hand
side and all of the negatives (bad things that will happen if the decision is
made) on the right hand side.
JORDAN BELFORT
Basics of The Straight Line Persuasion System	9

-- 11 of 12 --

And then you have the invisible force, which is limiting beliefs
about buying, affecting whether the scale tips to the negative or
the positive.
Your goal is to “Tip the Scales,” to where the positives outweigh the
negatives and viceversa.
NEGATIVES
POSITIVES
When you’re going through the Straight Line from open to close, what
you’re doing is working to cross the Action Threshold by knocking out
negatives, building up positives, and changing their beliefs about buying
all at the same time without them even knowing it. That’s what the core of
selling is—to get someone to a point where they say “yes.”
JORDAN BELFORT
Basics of The Straight Line Persuasion System	10

-- 12 of 12 --
